---
title: "cmse410"
author: "Joseph Burke"
date: "2026-02-17"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown
```{r}
#install.packages("devtools")
devtools::install_github("xuranw/MuSiC")
install.packages("BisqueRNA")
library(BisqueRNA)
library(MuSiC)
if (!require("anndata", quietly = TRUE)) install.packages("anndata")
library(anndata)
if (!require("BiocManager", quietly = TRUE)) install.packages("BiocManager")
BiocManager::install("GEOquery")

library(GEOquery)

```

```{r}
path <- r"(C:\Users\Joseph Burke\Downloads\placenta_data.h5ad)"
sc_ref <- read_h5ad(path)


# Download the series information
gse_data <- getGEO("GSE174399", destdir = ".")

```



```{r}
# 3. Quick check of the data structure
print(sc_ref)

# Extract the expression matrix (The "Smoothie")
bulk_smoothie <- exprs(gse_data[[1]])

# Check it
head(bulk_smoothie[, 1:5])

# After loading sc_ref with anndata
# The 'obs' slot contains the metadata
table(sc_ref$obs$infection) 

# This should show a 'control' or 'uninfected' group of ~74,585 cells

# 1. Unpack the list (gse is usually the object name from your getGEO command)
# If you used: gse <- getGEO("GSE174399"), then:
gse_object <- gse[[1]]

# 2. Extract the Bulk Counts (The "Smoothie")
bulk_counts <- exprs(gse_object)

# 3. Extract the Metadata (The "Flavor Profile" - i.e., which sample is e7.5 vs e9.5)
bulk_metadata <- pData(gse_object)

# 4. Peek at your data
head(bulk_counts[, 1:5])
table(bulk_metadata$title) # This shows you how many samples you have of each type
```

```{r}
# 1. Unpack the list (gse is usually the object name from your getGEO command)
# If you used: gse <- getGEO("GSE174399"), then:
gse_object <- gse[[1]]

# 2. Extract the Bulk Counts (The "Smoothie")
bulk_counts <- exprs(gse_object)

# 3. Extract the Metadata (The "Flavor Profile" - i.e., which sample is e7.5 vs e9.5)
bulk_metadata <- pData(gse_object)

# 4. Peek at your data
head(bulk_counts[, 1:5])
table(bulk_metadata$title) # This shows you how many samples you have of each type
```


```{r}
# 1. Unpack the GEO list correctly
gse_object <- gse_data[[1]] 

# 2. Extract the Bulk Counts (The "Smoothie")
bulk_counts <- exprs(gse_object)

# 3. Extract the Metadata
bulk_metadata <- pData(gse_object)

# 4. Filter the Single-Cell Reference for 'Normal' cells
# We only want the control cells to act as our 'unmixed' reference


# Find common genes
common <- intersect(sc_genes, bulk_genes)

print(paste("Number of overlapping genes:", length(common)))

# If this number is low (e.g., < 100), we need to convert IDs.

# Check what the actual names are
print(unique(sc_ref$obs$infection))

# Switch to the correct label (usually 'uninfected' in this specific dataset)
# Or use the 'disease' column which usually says 'normal' or 'Patsy'


# If that still fails, try filtering by the 'disease' column
# sc_normal <- sc_ref[sc_ref$obs$disease == "normal", ]

print(paste("Number of Normal Cells:", nrow(sc_normal$obs)))

# Use the direct accessors for AnnData objects in R
sc_genes <- as.character(sc_normal$var_names)
bulk_genes <- rownames(bulk_counts)

# Find common genes
common <- intersect(sc_genes, bulk_genes)
print(paste("Number of overlapping genes:", length(common)))

# --- CHUNK: Filtering ---
# Use 'uninfected' or 'normal' based on the Hoo et al. metadata
# Let's assume 'uninfected' for now, but check table(sc_ref$obs$infection)
sc_normal <- sc_ref[sc_ref$obs$infection == "uninfected", ]

# Verify count
print(paste("Number of Normal Cells:", nrow(sc_normal)))

# --- CHUNK: Overlap & Matrix Prep ---
# Extract names safely
sc_genes <- as.character(sc_normal$var_names)
bulk_genes <- rownames(bulk_counts)

common <- intersect(sc_genes, bulk_genes)
print(paste("Number of overlapping genes:", length(common)))

# IMPORTANT: MuSiC and Bisque need a matrix, not a 'double' object
# We convert the sparse matrix to a standard matrix for the overlapping genes
sc_matrix <- as.matrix(t(sc_normal$X)) # Transpose because AnnData is Cells x Genes
sc_matrix <- sc_matrix[common, ]

```


```{r}
# This command automatically finds and downloads the Series Matrix for you
gse_data <- getGEO("GSE174399", destdir = ".")

# This extracts the 'Smoothie' matrix from that file
bulk_counts <- exprs(gse_data[[1]])

head(bulk_counts)
```
